task_completion.description: Open the layer properties and navigate to the Symbology tab
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Properties' dialog for the 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' layer is open, and the 'Symbology' tab is active.
In the Symbology tab, 'Intervals' (or equivalent graduated colors/quantities option) is selected from the list of symbology types.

task_completion.description: Select POP_EST as the classification field
task_completion.visual_evidence: The classification field dropdown menu shows 'POP_EST' as the selected field.

task_completion.description: Configure Equal Intervals with exactly 5 classes
task_completion.visual_evidence: The classification method is set to 'Equal Intervals', the number of classes/intervals is set to '5', and exactly 5 rows/ranges are visible in the symbology classes table.

task_completion.description: Apply the symbology so the map canvas updates
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main gvSIG map canvas shows the world map with countries colored according to a graduated color ramp, displaying at least 4 distinct colors.

task_completion.description: Save the project to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' dialog shows the path '/home/ga/gvsig_data/projects/population_choropleth.gvsproj', or the main gvSIG window title bar updates to show 'population_choropleth.gvsproj'.

integrity.description: The agent used the gvSIG Desktop GUI to configure the symbology
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through gvSIG menus and dialogs (like Layer Properties), rather than just typing commands in a terminal or editing the .gvsproj XML file in a text editor.

integrity.description: The map rendering is genuine within the gvSIG application
integrity.visual_evidence: The choropleth map is visible inside the gvSIG map view window with standard gvSIG toolbars and interface elements present, not an opened image file in an image viewer.

integrity.description: The agent is using gvSIG Desktop
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window clearly belongs to gvSIG Desktop, identifiable by its icons, layout, and window title.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target field for classification is 'POP_EST'. The symbology type must be 'Intervals' using the 'Equal Intervals' method with exactly 5 classes. The final map should display at least 4 distinct colors. The project must be saved to '/home/ga/gvsig_data/projects/population_choropleth.gvsproj'.
